meta.snapshot: Launch-window meta (curated ~9 days post-launch, late May 2026).
meta.disclaimer: Launch-window snapshot, not a settled meta. Rankings will shift as players grind and post tunes. Re-verify high-cost buys against in-game leaderboards and the Find Tuning Setups marketplace.
meta.data_origin: Seeded from curated guide; refreshable via free web search.

cars.name: Ferrari FXX-K Evo
cars.strengths: Cleanest R-Class consensus; superb stock speed, handling & braking; only improves tuned.
cars.weaknesses: Power can overwhelm you on tight tracks.
cars.tune_summary: Road/grip baseline. Mech balance ~0.60. Add downforce for touge; brakes + front tyre width before power.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Aston Martin Valkyrie
cars.strengths: One of the fastest cars at top speed (default 9.2); handles well tuned.
cars.tune_summary: Road/grip baseline; longer final drive for straights, shorter for technical circuits.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: McLaren P1
cars.strengths: Lightning top speed for road/street.
cars.weaknesses: Comparatively slow acceleration; weaker on very twisty courses.
cars.tune_summary: Road/grip baseline; favour speed-zone gearing on open layouts.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Mercedes-AMG One
cars.strengths: Perfect 10 acceleration; active aero dominant on technical high-speed circuits. Meta S2 road pick.
cars.tune_summary: Road/grip baseline; mech balance 0.60; lean on aero in fast corners.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: GR GT Prototype (Toyota)
cars.strengths: PC Gamer's single favourite; cover car. Slicks + widened track = exceptionally planted. Standout value.
cars.tune_summary: Road/grip baseline; already planted — minimal alignment tweaks needed.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Koenigsegg Jesko
cars.strengths: Leads S2 in raw top speed; best for long straights / speed zones.
cars.tune_summary: Top-speed gearing (long final drive); soften to settle the rear on corner entry.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Nissan GT-R NISMO
cars.strengths: Go-to S1. AWD traction, ~600 hp, quick off the line; ideal for bend-heavy tracks. Base game, no DLC.
cars.tune_summary: Road/grip baseline; AWD diff ~70% rear; mech balance 0.60.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Porsche 911 GT3 RS
cars.strengths: Quintessential street racer; slicks + wider base = perfect for tight tracks.
cars.tune_summary: Road/grip baseline; shorter final drive for technical city/mountain routes.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Porsche 911 Turbo S
cars.strengths: 10 launch stat, strong acceleration; accessible and very tunable.
cars.weaknesses: Understeers if front tyres left stock.
cars.tune_summary: Road/grip baseline; widen front tyres + brakes before power.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Toyota GR Supra
cars.strengths: Cleanest modern-JDM A pick; most flexible platform (road/drift/CC). Well-tuned it punches into S1.
cars.tune_summary: Road/grip baseline; the workhorse — tune to the event.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: BMW M2 Competition Coupe
cars.strengths: Traditional A option with better speed/handling balance.
cars.tune_summary: Road/grip baseline; balanced springs, slight forward brake bias.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Volkswagen Golf R
cars.strengths: Cited as strongest AWD all-rounder for B-Class road.
cars.tune_summary: Road/grip baseline; AWD diff bias rear; keep it under the 700 PI ceiling.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Toyota 2000GT
cars.strengths: Standout B-Class pick.
cars.weaknesses: Barn Find — not buyable until found.
cars.tune_summary: Road/grip baseline; classic chassis — modest power, lean on handling.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Nissan GT-R Black Edition (R35) 'Forza Edition'
cars.strengths: Consensus fastest drag car. Pre-tuned AWD, drag tyres + chute, 10/10/10 speed/accel/launch, ~6s quarter mile.
cars.weaknesses: Near-uncontrollable in corners — straight-line only. Forza Edition, can't be bought outright.
cars.tune_summary: Drag tune (comes pre-tuned). Top gear long; tune 1st gear iteratively on the strip.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Koenigsegg Jesko
cars.strengths: Top-speed king for speed-trap / half-mile pulls.
cars.weaknesses: Not as quick off the line as the AWD GT-R FE.
cars.tune_summary: Top-speed build: longest viable final drive; minimal downforce.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Hennessey Venom F5
cars.strengths: Raw-power top-speed pick (community clocked 315+ mph).
cars.weaknesses: Less forgiving than the GT-R FE.
cars.tune_summary: Top-speed gearing; controllable wheelspin off the line.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Mazda MX-5 Miata 'Forza Edition'
cars.strengths: Best accessible drag pick; lightweight AWD. Get accel + launch to 10 via tune.
cars.weaknesses: Disagreement vs GT-R FE on outright pace.
cars.tune_summary: Drag tyres; hard-launch diff with controllable wheelspin; long top gear.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Toyota Supra RZ
cars.strengths: Cheapest credible drag starter.
cars.tune_summary: Drag tune; prioritise launch + 1st-gear tuning on the strip.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Formula Drift #151 Toyota GR Supra
cars.strengths: Glides and smokes even stock; FD cars outperform any B-class drift build.
cars.tune_summary: Drift tune: front tyre near-max PSI, rear low; max front camber, rear ~0, front toe-out; locked accel diff; forward brake bias.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Lotus Evija 'Forza Edition'
cars.strengths: S2 drift specialist — electric instant torque.
cars.tune_summary: Drift tune; manage instant torque with rear PSI + brake bias.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Formula Drift #64 Nissan Z
cars.strengths: Scores high, holds angle, less twitchy than electric drift cars — good for beginners.
cars.weaknesses: Edged out by the #151 Supra at the top.
cars.tune_summary: Drift tune; slightly softer than the #151 for forgiveness.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Nissan Silvia Spec-R
cars.strengths: Unassuming but swings around corners; strong engine + wide drift tyres; clears most challenges.
cars.weaknesses: Outscored by Formula Drift cars in open zones.
cars.tune_summary: Drift tune; wide rear drift tyres; stiff rear springs; rear toe-out.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Mazda MX-5 Miata RF
cars.strengths: RWD, well-balanced; loves to go sideways and chains drifts without spinning. Any MX-5 works.
cars.tune_summary: Drift tune; forgiving balance — great learner drifter.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Toyota AE86 Sprinter Trueno 'Forza Edition'
cars.strengths: The 'correct' car for Mt. Haruna touge by physics (~980 kg, high-revving).
cars.tune_summary: Stiff rear springs, rear toe-out, 1.5-way or open LSD; keep power secondary to balance.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Subaru BRZ 'Forza Edition'
cars.acquisition: Aftermarket spot at Sotoyama Ski Resort (not Autoshow)
cars.strengths: Perfect 10 off-road rating; a beast in both dirt and cross-country when tuned.
cars.weaknesses: Must be located at the Aftermarket spot.
cars.tune_summary: Rally tune: 22-27 PSI, soft springs near minimum, ride height 5-7in off bump stops, center diff 60-70% rear.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Ford #4 Focus RS (rally)
cars.strengths: PC Gamer default: scrappy, high speed + accel + off-road handling.
cars.tune_summary: Rally tune; soft, high ride height; shorter gearing.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Subaru Impreza WRX STI
cars.strengths: Iconic, dirt-cheap; good general stat spread with headroom before hitting A-rank.
cars.weaknesses: Needs a rally conversion for higher classes.
cars.tune_summary: Rally tune; AWD; build up to the B ceiling, then bump to A when ready.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Mitsubishi Lancer Evolution VIII MR
cars.acquisition: Autoshow (Welcome Pack version pre-tuned to A)
cars.strengths: Near-identical to the Impreza, slightly higher stats across the board.
cars.tune_summary: Rally tune; Welcome Pack copy arrives pre-tuned to A-rank.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Dodge Viper 'Forza Edition' / GTS ACR FE
cars.strengths: Best S1+ cross-country: incredibly fast and lightweight, nothing comparable right now.
cars.weaknesses: Premium/VIP locked. Free alternative: Subaru BRZ FE.
cars.tune_summary: Cross-country tune: suspension for stability + durability; soft, raised ride height.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Mercedes-Benz X-Class
cars.strengths: Best A-Class CC pick; consistent across all CC track types.
cars.tune_summary: Cross-country tune; prioritise stability and suspension travel.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Ford F-450 (Super Duty)
cars.strengths: Best choice for any race in D-B; consistent across all cross-country tracks.
cars.tune_summary: Cross-country tune; soft long-travel suspension; durability first.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Ford F-150 Raptor R
cars.strengths: Huge power and great for ramming.
cars.weaknesses: Ugly handling on paper — tune handling first, then top speed.
cars.tune_summary: Cross-country tune; fix handling/stability before chasing top speed.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)

cars.name: Toyota Land Cruiser
cars.strengths: Reliable all-terrain.
cars.weaknesses: Needs accel + braking tuned for bendy tracks.
cars.tune_summary: Cross-country tune; raise accel + braking for technical CC layouts.
cars.code_status: No verified public code — build in-game via Find Tuning Setups (see Tuning sheet)
